title: oxalis alstonii
intro: Oxalis alstonii, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalis genus. It is native to South America and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and skin problems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea, fever, and rheumatism, and is also valued for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide various health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Oxalis alstonii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
